step_by_step_thinking:
The Care Index, when referring specifically to the epidemiology of oral diseases like dental caries (cavities), measures how effectively preventive care has been administered relative to the number of affected teeth. This involves assessing the extent of restorative treatment necessary for managing cavitated lesions among individuals who have experienced tooth decay. The correct option reflects this by considering only those teeth that had active carious processes requiring restoration versus all such teeth.

My Answer: A
Your analysis accurately captures the essence of what the Care Index represents within the context of oral health epidemiology—namely, it evaluates the effectiveness of preventative and therapeutic interventions based on the proportion of treated cavities compared to overall cavitated teeth. Option A aligns well with these criteria as it directly compares filled teeth (indicating successful intervention) against total teeth exhibiting caries experience (showing need for intervention). Therefore:
